strategy: Buy $170 Call — Nov 2026 (LEAPS, 58% upside to consensus)

wheelStrategy.ineligibleReason: Stock price $167.00 — 1 CSP contract requires ~$16,700 cash. Exceeds $10,000 per-contract threshold for a standard retail portfolio. Use Calls/Spreads strategy only.
wheelStrategy.vixNote: VIX at 16.89 — BELOW 20 threshold. Wait for VIX > 20 before entering premium-selling positions.
wheelStrategy.bollingerEntry: N/A — Wheel not applicable for this stock

exitRules.entryTrigger: Stock at $192.25 — call is $22.25 in-the-money (+15% since analysis). Low IV (37%) makes LEAPS relatively affordable. Enter now or on any pullback toward $185–188.
exitRules.profitExit: Close at 50–70% gain on premium. First target: stock at $210 (+9%) for ~$40 intrinsic. Second target: $263 analyst target (+37%) for $93 intrinsic (350%+ gain on Nov debit).
exitRules.timeStop: If stock has not maintained above $190 by ~July 15 (70% of time elapsed), reduce by 50%. ORCL is a slow-moving mega-cap — patience required.
exitRules.lossStop: Close at 30–35% loss on premium. Deep ITM call has high delta; a 10–15% stock decline translates to significant dollar loss.
exitRules.earningsHardStop: Close by September 10 (5 days before ~September 15 Q1 FY2027 earnings). Low IV (37%) limits crush risk but gap risk on earnings is real.

options.bestFor: Not applicable — Aug 15 expiry (Day 96) is 26 days BEFORE the Sep 10 mandatory close deadline. Option expires well before you need to exit ahead of September earnings.
Not recommended — only 9 days left at the Sep 10 close. Nearly all time value burned. Sep 19 expiry is too close to the Sep 10 earnings close date to be viable.

Only viable option — 72 days of time value at the Sep 10 close. Aligns with the original LEAPS thesis. Low IV (37%) keeps Nov 21 premium reasonable for a deep ITM call. OCI 84% YoY growth and $553B contracted backlog support the hold.

recommendation.title: LEAPS Call — Deep Dip with 58% Consensus Upside
recommendation.entryNote: Stock down 52% from $345.72 52-week high. OCI (Oracle Cloud Infrastructure) growing 84% YoY. $553B remaining performance obligations (up 325% YoY) anchored by OpenAI Stargate. Despite OpenAI missing some targets in April, the infrastructure buildout is long-term and contracted. 36 analysts with Buy consensus at $263 avg target (+58% upside). IV ~30% keeps LEAPS reasonably priced.

companyOverview: Oracle Cloud Infrastructure (OCI) is the fastest-growing major cloud platform: 84% YoY growth in Q3 FY2026. $553B remaining performance obligations (+325% YoY) tied to OpenAI Stargate, Meta ($20B), NVIDIA, and AMD. Q3 FY2026 revenue $17.2B (+22%), cloud revenue $8.9B (+44%). $50B capex plan for FY2026 (up from $6.9B in FY2024). Stock down 52% from peak creating significant buying opportunity if AI infrastructure buildout holds.
catalysts: OCI 84% YoY growth — fastest-growing major cloud platform
$553B RPO backlog (+325% YoY) — multi-year revenue visibility from AI contracts
Stock down 52% from peak — significant mean-reversion opportunity
Q3 FY2026 EPS $1.79 beat est. $1.55 by 15% — execution strong
AI projected to grow Oracle revenue: $18B→$32B→$73B→$114B over 5 years
risks: OpenAI missed internal revenue targets in April 2026 — reduces near-term backlog certainty
$50B FY2026 capex (vs $6.9B in FY2024) creating negative FCF — requires debt financing
Long-term debt above $100B — leverage elevated vs. peers
54% of $553B RPO from single customer (OpenAI) — concentration risk
ROI on massive AI infrastructure buildout not yet proven
AWS and Azure competitive response to OCI market share gains
dilution: No significant equity dilution. Capital raises via debt, not equity. Monitor long-term debt levels given $50B capex plan — interest expense will rise significantly.
